headline: Fortnite is getting a bunch of AI-powered ‘personas’
summary: Epic Games introduces AI-powered voices and characters to Fortnite. Creators can use 36 pre-made AI NPCs starting July 30th. This feature aims to enhance player experiences with dynamic AI interactions.

headline: Samsung’s 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper than usual
summary: Samsung's 55-inch Frame TV is currently on sale, offering $200 in savings. This unique TV displays art when not in use, transforming into a framed picture. It features a matte finish and art-like bezels for aesthetic appeal.

headline: Netflix says around 300 titles used generative AI
summary: Netflix reveals approximately 300 titles on its platform utilized generative AI. Most AI use occurred in post-production for efficiency and cost-saving. The company aims to deliver higher quality output faster and more affordably.

headline: Why are people buying so many CDs?
summary: CD sales are surprisingly on the rise, increasing 16% year-over-year in the US. Fans are buying CDs as an affordable way to support their favorite artists. 16.3 million CDs were sold in the first half of 2026, according to Luminate.

headline: God of War Show to Recast Kratos After Ryan Hurst Tears Bicep While Filming Stunt
summary: Ryan Hurst, playing Kratos in the God of War show, tore his bicep during a stunt. Production for the Amazon MGM and Sony Pictures series is now on hold. The studios have been forced to recast the iconic Kratos character.

headline: Game of Thrones: War for Westeros Release Date Delayed to Early 2027
summary: PlaySide Studios has postponed the release of Game of Thrones: War for Westeros. The real-time strategy game is now expected in early 2027. The game is set within the popular HBO series universe.

headline: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Director Reveals Why Ubisoft Ditched Edward's Hidden Blade Combat
summary: Director Richard Knight explains changes to Edward Kenway's combat in Black Flag Resynced. Ubisoft removed some of Edward's original hidden blade fighting moves. Fans shouldn't lose hope for the return of these moves in the future.

headline: Google Pixel 11 Specs Leak Ahead of Next Month’s Unveiling
summary: Early leaks reveal potential specs for the upcoming Google Pixel 11 lineup. New Pixel 11, Pro, and Pro Fold models are expected to launch next month. Reports suggest a variety of new colors and a price increase across the series.

headline: Burnham to promise new path for UK when he becomes new Labour leader
summary: Burnham will outline his vision for government in a speech on Friday. He is set to become the new Prime Minister next week. He aims to promise a 'new path' for the United Kingdom under his leadership.

headline: Burnham's 'Manchesterism' got him to No 10 - but will it work for the UK?
summary: Economics editor Faisal Islam examines Burnham's 'Manchesterism' approach. It questions if the north-west city's model can apply to the entire UK. Burnham's regional success has propelled him towards No 10.

headline: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
summary: China criticizes the UK government's decision to nationalize British Steel. The UK asserts the move safeguards 'a vital national capability'. The firm has been taken into public hands by the government.

headline: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
summary: The US has conducted new strikes in the region, escalating tensions. Iran reports that these strikes targeted civilian infrastructure. US forces also boarded a ship in the Strait of Hormuz during the heightened situation.
